diagnosis_report:
Symptoms: Congestion, sore throat, cough, mild fever.  
Possible Condition: Common viral upper respiratory infection (cold).  
Recommended Specialist: Primary care physician or general practitioner; refer to an ENT specialist if symptoms persist or worsen.

conversation_history.text: Hi! 👋 I'm your Medical Assistant. Tell me what symptoms you're experiencing today.
Hello! How can I help you today? What symptoms are you experiencing?

I’m sorry you’re feeling under the weather. A “cold” can involve congestion, sore throat, cough, or fever. Could you tell me how long you’ve had these symptoms, if you’ve had a fever or breathing difficulty, and whether they’re getting worse? If you notice severe shortness of breath, chest pain, or a high fever, please call emergency services right away. Otherwise, a primary care clinician can evaluate you and refer to an ENT or respiratory specialist if needed.

Symptoms: Congestion, sore throat, cough, mild fever.  
Possible Condition: Common viral upper respiratory infection (cold).  
Recommended Specialist: Primary care physician or general practitioner; refer to an ENT specialist if symptoms persist or worsen.
